senses.definition: to be occupied by a large number of things

examples.x: The suitcase was packed so tightly that it was bursting at the seams, barely able to close.
The theater was filled to capacity, with the audience spilling into the aisles, making it seem like the place was bursting at the seams.
The company experienced rapid growth, and their office space soon became bursting at the seams, necessitating a move to a larger location.
The storage room was so cluttered that boxes were stacked on top of each other, ready to burst at the seams.
The cake was generously frosted, with layers of cream and filling, almost bursting at the seams with sweetness.

senses.definition: completely direct and without any twists and deviations

examples.x: The highway stretched ahead, straight as an arrow, leading to our destination without any twists or turns.
His posture was impeccable, standing tall and straight as an arrow, exuding confidence and poise.
The architect designed the building with clean lines and sharp angles, resulting in a modern structure that stood straight as an arrow.
The gymnast performed a flawless routine, executing each movement with precision and landing straight as an arrow.

senses.definition: in an environment that is not enclosed

examples.x: The campers set up their tents in the open, surrounded by nature and under the starry sky.
The garden party was held out in the open, with guests mingling on the spacious lawn.

senses.definition: to forcefully make a path through a crowded or obstructed area

examples.x: The determined athlete elbowed his way through the defenders, making a clear path towards the goal.
In the bustling marketplace, shoppers had to elbow their way through the crowd to reach the popular stalls.
The ambitious entrepreneur elbowed her way into the industry, overcoming fierce competition and establishing herself as a key player.
The concertgoers excitedly elbowed their way to the front row, eager to get the best view of the performance.
The politician elbowed his way through the crowd, shaking hands and engaging with supporters in a campaign rally.

senses.definition: enough space that enables people to move easily

examples.x: The small apartment felt cramped, lacking the elbow room needed to freely move around.
The meeting room was spacious, providing participants with ample elbow room to collaborate and express their ideas.
The children were excited to have a large backyard, giving them plenty of elbow room to play games and run around.
The new office layout was designed with open spaces and wide aisles, allowing employees to have more elbow room and foster a productive environment.
The conference hall was filled to capacity, leaving attendees with limited elbow room, making it difficult to take notes or participate comfortably.

senses.definition: in a place that is far away from cities, towns, or anywhere that is occupied by people

examples.x: The cabin was tucked away in the middle of nowhere, surrounded by dense forests and miles of untouched nature.
After hours of driving, they found themselves stranded with a flat tire in the middle of nowhere, with no sign of civilization in sight.
The tiny village was located in the middle of nowhere, accessible only by a long, winding road through the mountains.
The hikers set up their tents in the middle of nowhere, enjoying the tranquility and solitude of the remote wilderness.
The farmhouse was hidden in the middle of nowhere, providing a peaceful retreat from the noise and busyness of city life.

senses.definition: an area that is near a place in which someone resides

examples.x: I haven't seen him around this neck of the woods for quite some time.
The new bookstore is a great addition to our neck of the woods, providing a gathering place for book lovers.
We're having a neighborhood barbecue next weekend, so make sure to invite everyone from our neck of the woods.
The local news station covers stories from our neck of the woods, keeping us informed about events in our community.
Our annual festival is a highlight of the year for everyone in this neck of the woods, bringing together residents from nearby towns and villages.

senses.definition: a small or confined room with very limited space for movement

examples.x: The studio apartment was so small that there was hardly enough room to swing a cat.
The crowded subway during rush hour leaves little room to swing a cat, let alone find a seat.
The tiny conference room provided very little room to swing a cat, making the meeting feel cramped.
The hotel bathroom was so cramped that there was barely enough room to swing a cat in it.
In the crowded marketplace, there was barely any room to swing a cat as people jostled past each other.

senses.definition: all possible places, particularly those that are out of sight or reach

examples.x: The detective searched every nook and cranny of the crime scene, hoping to find a clue that would lead to the culprit.
The cleaning crew meticulously cleaned the house, making sure to reach every nook and cranny to remove dust and dirt.
The treasure hunters explored the old mansion, examining every nook and cranny in their quest for hidden riches.
The architect designed the house with clever storage solutions, utilizing every nook and cranny to maximize space.
The archaeologists excavated the ancient ruins, carefully documenting and studying every nook and cranny to uncover insights into the past.

senses.definition: without sufficient space for one to move freely

examples.x: The concert was so popular that the audience was packed like sardines in the stadium.
During rush hour, the subway train is often packed like sardines with commuters trying to get to work.
The bus was so overcrowded that passengers were squashed in like sardines, struggling to find breathing room.
The small elevator was filled to capacity, with people squashed like sardines, eager to reach their desired floor.
At the music festival, the crowd in front of the stage was packed in like sardines, creating an electric atmosphere.

senses.definition: a familiar place or location, particularly one where someone often goes to or spends time in

examples.x: The local park became his stomping ground, where he would jog every morning and meet his friends for picnics.
The bustling caf√© down the street is considered the students' stamping ground, where they gather to study and socialize.
The small coastal town serves as a popular stomping ground for surfers, drawn to its excellent waves and welcoming community.
After work, the neighborhood pub becomes their regular stamping ground, where they unwind and enjoy a few drinks.
The downtown area transforms into a vibrant stomping ground during the annual street festival, attracting visitors from far and wide.

word_list.pronunciation: f…Ļňą…õ É ňĆa ä…ĺ…ôv ňĆ…õsdňą Ćb…ôljňĆuňź

senses.definition: having just returned from a specific place
(of one's posture) in a straight and rigid position

examples.x: I am sure she is stiff as a ramrod.
The guard stood straight as a ramrod.

senses.definition: to be down on one's hands and knees

examples.x: The toddler was on all fours, exploring the room and crawling towards his toys.
The hiker lost his balance and fell, ending up on all fours as he regained his footing on the slippery trail.
After an exhausting race, the runner collapsed on all fours, trying to catch their breath and recover.
